title: Speculative prefetch streams are never cancelled; concurrent count stacks past 6 under navigation
description: maybePrefetch (store.ts:411-428, MAX_PREFETCH=6 at :126) launches up to 6 concurrent 3072-token generations per settled visible screen (stream.ts:154). Navigating away prevents NEW prefetches (activeScreenId guard) but never aborts launched ones; tapping into a done prefetched child immediately fans out 6 more. Fix: abort stale speculative streams on navigation; consider prefetching top 1-2 actions first. Existing mitigations to preserve: speculative tool-round refusal (store.ts:215-217), actionIndex reuse.
close_reason: cancelStalePrefetches in setActiveScreen: aborts speculative streams whose parent left the screen; cancelled entries silently error and regenerate on tap (same path as tool-refused prefetches). Tapped screens protected via speculative:false flip. Kept MAX_PREFETCH=6: spend now bounded to visible screen.

title: Summoned-app IDs collide on 24-char truncation, silently discarding new requests
description: routeCommand truncates the command to 24 chars (GenOS.tsx:531) before summonApp slugs it into the app id (apps.ts:116). 'plan my trip to japan in april' and '...in december' → same summon- id; launch() sees an existing session and resumes the old screen, silently dropping the new request. Fix: slug from the full text (or hash suffix); truncate only the display name.
close_reason: summonAppForCommand: id slugged from the full command text, only the display name truncates to 24 chars. Punctuation-only collisions remain (slug strips non-alnum) - acceptable ceiling. 4 tests.

title: Switcher eagerly mounts a full live Renderer (incl. Google Maps WebViews) per running app
description: Each switcher card renders <Renderer isStreaming={false}> at 376x800 for every running app simultaneously (Switcher.tsx:16-20, plain horizontal ScrollView, no lazy mounting). A MapView in any preview spins up a real react-native-webview Google Maps embed; images all fetch. isStreaming=false on possibly mid-stream content can also render half-parsed programs. Fix: static snapshot/preview or lazy-mount visible cards only.
close_reason: FlatList windowing (initialNumToRender 3, windowSize 3, removeClippedSubviews), RenderPreviewContext swaps WebView map embeds for flat placeholders in miniatures, and previews of mid-generation screens now pass isStreaming honestly. 2 tests.

title: KeyGate token field lacks secureTextEntry / autofill hardening
description: KeyGate.tsx:56 TextInput has autoCapitalize=none + autoCorrect=false but no secureTextEntry, autoComplete=off, importantForAutofill=no, or textContentType. One-time BYOK paste of the user's own key on their own device — Low-Medium. Fix: concealed entry with explicit reveal toggle (keep paste verification usable).
close_reason: secureTextEntry default-on with Show/Hide reveal toggle (accessible), autoComplete=off, importantForAutofill=no, textContentType=none. 2 tests.

title: A11y: alt text discarded end-to-end; chips/tabs/list rows lack roles and selected state
description: contract.tsx:32 defines alt but createImg drops it (media.tsx:12) AND all 4 Img call sites pass only .src (cupertino 192/522, material 184/494). ImageBlock (contract.tsx:344) has no alt in its schema at all. Only 2 accessibility props exist in the whole ui/ tree (both switches); Chips/Tabs/ListItem Pressables lack accessibilityRole and selected state. Fix: thread alt through both renderers, add roles/states to interactive elements.
close_reason: alt threaded end-to-end (createImg + all 4 call sites, incl. placeholder state; ImageBlock uses caption as stand-in since its schema lacks alt); chips=button+selected, tabs=tab+selected, actionable ListItems and Buttons=button in BOTH renderers. 5 a11y tests across cupertino + material.

title: Cached @OS command screens execute once, then poison the action cache (repeat navigation = blank screen)
description: Prompt-documented @OS(...) replies execute via a once-per-screen-id guard (executedOsCommands, GenOS.tsx:393-398) and the screen is pulled from the stack — but resolveAction cached its id in actionIndex forever (store.ts:377). Repeating the same navigation-shaped request later (e.g. 'take me home please', which routeCommand's regexes miss) reuses the cached id: the command is skipped and a dead screen is pushed. Fix: never cache screens whose result is an osCommand (or clear the actionIndex entry when one executes).
close_reason: forgetScreen(id) drops actionIndex/deepLinkIndex/appHomeIndex entries when a screen resolves to an @OS command, so repeat navigation-shaped requests regenerate and re-execute instead of hitting a dead cached frame. 2 tests.

title: Lifecycle behavior untested; coverage denominator excludes core runtime files
description: Coverage 47.93/33.46/37.22 reproduces exactly, but no collectCoverageFrom is configured, so stream.ts, GenOS.tsx, and shell/ are excluded from the denominator entirely — true coverage is materially lower. No tests for session cleanup, cancellation, timeouts, prefetch, tool caps, KeyGate, a11y. Tests live at __tests__/ (repo root). Fix: add collectCoverageFrom and lifecycle tests alongside the cleanup/timeout fixes.
close_reason: collectCoverageFrom now measures all of src (minus generated prompt) - honest denominator 59/44/54/61 vs the old 47.9% over a partial tree; coverage floors (55/40/50/57) ratchet against regression; npm run check = typecheck + gated suite; 18 suites / 78 tests (was 4/22) incl. end-to-end shell smoke, telemetry opt-out proof, and lifecycle/timeout/tool-cap/trust-boundary seams. README documents the gate.

title: All user-entered form state is destroyed on every navigation
description: react-lang's form store is per-Renderer-mount (useMemo createStore) and exposes initialState/onStateUpdate as the persistence seam — GenOS passes neither (GenOS.tsx:637-642), and only the top screen is mounted. Back/forward loses everything typed. Fix: keep per-screen state keyed by screen id and wire initialState/onStateUpdate.
close_reason: GenOS keeps a per-screen form-state map: onStateUpdate captures committed values, initialState (mount-stable via useMemo on topId) re-seeds on revisit; closeSession sweeps entries for evicted screens. Ceiling: mid-typing (un-blurred) values still lost - react-lang only reports committed state. Seam test drives the round trip.

title: Model-produced URLs dispatched to Linking.openURL without scheme validation
description: handleAction (GenOS.tsx:418; actual call :433-434) passes every non-genos:// URL string to Linking.openURL. Model output is tainted (web_search snippets reach model context), so tel:/sms:/mailto:/app-specific schemes and phishing hosts dispatch on a user tap whose label the model also controls. intent:// is NOT parsed as Intent (RN uses ACTION_VIEW/Uri.parse) — that part of the audit was overstated. Fix: parse URL, allowlist http/https, confirm before leaving the app.
close_reason: isSafeExternalUrl allowlists http(s) only (whitespace-smuggling guarded); user confirms the destination host before Linking.openURL (Alert natively, confirm() on web); blocked schemes toast. parseGenosUrl moved to urls.ts and brought under test. 8 tests.

title: Any HTTP 403 permanently deletes the user's stored API key
description: stream.ts:158 treats 403 like 401 → markRejected (config.ts:94-99) nulls the key AND erases SecureStore/localStorage; response body is never read. 403 also covers WAF/entitlement/region blocks with a perfectly valid key — user gets an unrecoverable re-enter loop with a misleading 'key rejected' message. Fix: only 401 destroys the key; surface 403 body text as a distinct error.
close_reason: Only 401 wipes the stored key; 403 keeps it and surfaces the provider's response body as a distinct retryable error. 3 tests incl. stale-401 no-op guard.

title: 11 moderate npm advisories (transitive postcss + uuid via Expo)
description: npm audit --omit=dev: 11 moderate, no high/critical. Roots: postcss <8.5.10 (GHSA-qx2v-qp2m-jg93), uuid <11.1.1 (GHSA-w5hq-g745-h8pq); other 9 are Expo packages depending on them. npm audit fix --force would jump Expo 54→57 (breaks Expo Go pin) — needs SDK-compatible remediation (overrides or wait for Expo patch releases).
close_reason: npm overrides: postcss ^8.5.10 (global, same-major) and uuid ^11.1.1 scoped to xcode. 0 vulnerabilities in prod and full tree; 74 tests, typecheck, and expo-doctor 18/18 all pass - no Expo 57 jump needed.

title: web_search results enter model context with no trust boundary (prompt injection drives generated UI)
description: formatWebResults (search.ts:98-107) concatenates attacker-controllable Exa titles/snippets verbatim into a tool message (pushed stream.ts:286); the system prompt tells the model to compose the screen 'strictly from' them. A malicious page can steer the UI into phishing CTAs, chaining into the openURL (appless-8l7) and image-beacon (appless-gds) sinks. Fix: delimit/escape results, mark them untrusted in the prompt, cap snippet content.
close_reason: formatWebResults fences results as UNTRUSTED with an explicit ignore-instructions directive; sanitizeWebText strips delimiter sentinels so pages cannot fake-close the fence. Sink-side URL validation is defense-in-depth via 8l7/gds. 3 tests.

title: Streaming and Exa fetches have no absolute or idle timeout
description: The reader loop (stream.ts:175-176; getReader at :167) awaits indefinitely; same for webSearch fetch (search.ts:71). STALE_MS=30s only aborts a hung stream as a side effect of a user re-tap (reusable→retryScreen→startStream abort); never-revisited speculative prefetch streams hang forever. Fix: AbortSignal.timeout-style absolute cap + idle timer between chunks; cancel reader in cleanup.
close_reason: createWatchdog (caller signal + 90s absolute + 15s idle) wired into streamRound; Exa fetch gets 20s absolute. Timeouts surface as retryable errors; caller aborts stay silent. 7 tests incl. hung-reader seam test.

title: EXPO_PUBLIC_* embeds Exa/Unsplash keys in any distributed build (no BYOK path exists for them)
description: config.ts:15/:16/:19 read EXPO_PUBLIC_UNSPLASH_ACCESS_KEY / EXPO_PUBLIC_EXA_API_KEY / EXPO_PUBLIC_CEREBRAS_API_KEY — inlined into the JS bundle at build time. Cerebras has a safe default path (in-app BYOK + SecureStore) and README scopes the env var to dev; but Exa/Unsplash env vars are the ONLY enablement path, so distributing a build with those features means shipping the keys. Fix: per-user entry (SecureStore) or a rate-limited proxy for Exa/Unsplash; README warning about bundle embedding. Audit cited config.ts:11 (wrong line — that is the base URL).
close_reason: README now warns EXPO_PUBLIC_* is bundle-inlined and dev-only, prescribes BYOK prompt for Cerebras and a proxy for Exa/Unsplash in distributed builds; same warning at the config.ts source. Skipped: in-app SecureStore entry for optional tool keys - demo scope, proxy is the right distribution answer.

title: Non-semantic image srcs load unrestricted (tracking beacons via prompt-injectable URLs)
description: useSemanticImage passes any non-/api/img src straight through (images.ts:110; fn at :86) and createImg loads it in <Image> (media.tsx:18). RN Image is a display-only sink, so real risk is tracking/beacon GETs to attacker-chosen hosts (concrete via web_search injection), not code execution. The /api/img path itself is well-guarded (parseImgUrl sanitizes+clamps). Fix: enforce /api/img or an https: host allowlist in useSemanticImage.
close_reason: Non-semantic srcs now load only from pipeline-produced hosts (images.unsplash.com, loremflickr.com over https); everything else renders the themed placeholder. Host-suffix and downgrade tricks covered in tests.

title: Prompt regeneration irreproducible from fresh clone; README misattributes it to @openuidev/cli
description: README.md:116 says @openuidev/cli generates the prompt, but it is not a dependency and npm run generate:prompt runs scripts/embed-prompt.mjs, which only EMBEDS a prompt produced by the sibling appless-os repo (default path at embed-prompt.mjs:13, loud exit :16-25). Committed artifact means clone+build works; only regeneration (needed for contract changes README encourages) breaks. Fix: document the real workflow or vendor the generation toolchain.
close_reason: Dropped the false @openuidev/cli dependency-table row; README now documents that the prompt is a committed artifact, that generate:prompt only embeds an externally produced prompt (sibling repo or explicit path), and that contract changes require regeneration.

title: Provider truncation (finish_reason=length) recorded but never surfaced
description: stream.ts:245 maps finish_reason 'length' to truncated; store.ts:240 patches it onto the screen; nothing anywhere reads Screen.truncated. With max_completion_tokens=3072, dense screens do get cut and render as complete with no retry affordance. Fix: show a 'screen was cut short — regenerate' affordance when truncated.
close_reason: Truncated done-screens now show a cut-short pill with tap-to-regenerate (retryScreen clears the flag). Data path tested in lifecycle suite.

title: Free-tier TPM quota blown by 6-way prefetch burst; raw JSON error shown to user
description: Live smoke test: entering a fresh key and launching one app immediately hit Cerebras token_quota_exceeded - each screen settle launches MAX_PREFETCH=6 speculative generations, each resending the ~6k-token system prompt (~7 full prompts/burst). Audit originally recommended prefetching top 1-2; bead 11t consciously kept 6, live evidence overturns that. Also streamRound surfaces the raw JSON error body on the retry screen instead of the .message field. Fix: MAX_PREFETCH=2; parse provider error JSON for the human message.
close_reason: MAX_PREFETCH 6->2 (live TPM evidence beats the kept-6 call from 11t); streamRound parses provider JSON error bodies and surfaces .message. Test pins the 429 quota shape from the live repro.

title: Closing an app never aborts its in-flight streams or evicts store state
description: closeSession (src/genos/GenOS.tsx:358) mutates only React state. The only .abort() in src is the retry-supersede path (store.ts:198). ScreenStore (store.ts:57) has no delete/evict API; actionIndex/appHomeIndex/deepLinkIndex/inflight maps only ever .set(). Orphaned streams run to completion (bounded: 3072 tok, <=3 tool rounds) and memory is never reclaimed. Fix: store-level closeApp(appId) that aborts inflight controllers for the app's screens, removes descendants, and evicts index entries. Note: appHomeIndex reuse is intentional cache — decide eviction policy consciously.
close_reason: Store-level closeApp(): aborts inflight controllers (incl. speculative children), evicts screens + actionIndex/appHomeIndex/deepLinkIndex entries; wired into GenOS closeSession. 5 lifecycle tests.

title: Any tap on a screen with form state leaks the full form snapshot (incl. password fields) into the LLM prompt
description: react-lang's triggerAction delivers the ENTIRE form-state snapshot when formName is undefined; useTap (ui/shared/actions.ts:14) and Chips (cupertino/components.tsx:593, material/components.tsx:573) pass formName=undefined for all non-form taps. GenOS.tsx:462 forwards ev.formState unconditionally; resolveAction (store.ts:346) treats any non-empty formState as a submission and serializes it into the Cerebras request (store.ts:368). Contract exposes Input type=password rendered with secureTextEntry (contract.tsx:475, shared/forms.ts:47) — masked on screen, plaintext to the API. Also defeats the prefetch cache for every such tap. Fix: only attach formState when the action names a form; strip/never-forward password-type values.
close_reason: Gate at GenOS boundary: extractFormValues only forwards a named form's slice, unwrapped, with password fields stripped (marked via componentType). Seam-tested against the real renderer.

title: No per-round cap/dedup on tool-call fan-out
description: stream.ts:283 (audit said :264) Promise.alls every model-returned call in a round. Real bounds exist: MAX_TOOL_ROUNDS=3 (stream.ts:40,:256), 3072-token round budget limits call JSON, speculative screens refuse tools, AbortSignal threads into Exa fetches. Remaining gap is per-round call count / dedup / query-length validation — Low-Medium, user's own Exa quota. Fix: cap calls per round (e.g. 3, matching the prompt), dedupe identical queries.
close_reason: Per-round executor guard: cap 3 executions (matching prompt), case-insensitive dedup, 200-char query limit. Skipped calls still get protocol-valid tool messages with honest ERROR content. Seam-tested through a mocked two-round SSE loop.
